mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.5%, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicates broad-based demand expansion, which compresses equity risk premiums and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in HOUST and PAYEMS do not produce forward returns significantly above the unconditional mean (t-stat < 1.96).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO surprises consistently coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns during peak capacity utilization phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.3% triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.3% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in retail sales and real GDP growth confirms robust aggregate demand, which sustains earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and GDPC1 fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals labor market strength that translates into higher consumer confidence and equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher during consecutive positive PAYEMS months compared to negative months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production rises while housing starts fall indicates sectoral imbalance and potential demand saturation, which increases equity volatility and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: This divergence pattern does not lead to negative 5-day SPX returns in >50% of observed instances.
